_comment: Every repo the agent can work on. The agent roster never changes per target — only these commands and conventions do. This realizes the language-agnostic envelope: add a Kotlin/Java target by adding an entry with its own check commands. ${base} is substituted with the --base git ref (default origin/main).

jira._comment: Required UNP fields for any ticket this system creates. App Name is REQUIRED by the UNP project — set per target/teammate at onboarding (see standards/jira.md for the full option list).

admin._conventions_note: Aligned to the team's ACTUAL practice (verified from git log): conventional-commit subject with the UNP id in trailing parens, e.g. `fix(security): harden webhook (UNP-8041)`. The ada plugin's `Refs: UNP-NNNN` trailer was aspirational and never used — the validator caught the drift on its first run.

checks.typecheck: npx nx run admin_backend:typecheck:remote && npx tsc -b ./apps/admin_client/tsconfig.json && npx nx run admin-types:typecheck:remote

forbiddenImports.name: client-imports-backend-runtime
forbiddenImports.message: admin_client must not import backend runtime — only the AppRouter TYPE via `import type` from @admin-backend is allowed.

omissionRules.name: trpc-route-without-controller-or-types
omissionRules.message: A tRPC route changed but no controller or shared-type change — verify the contract isn't half-wired.

sandbox._comment: Disposable training sandbox (dependency-free Node ESM). Built by training/setup-sandbox.mjs; exercised by training/run-training.mjs, which overrides `path` per task with --path <worktree>. This proves the whole envelope works on a non-admin target with no npm install.

forbiddenImports.name: no-fs-in-src
forbiddenImports.message: src/ must stay pure — no filesystem imports (a deliberate trap so a task can exercise the forbidden-import gate).
